title: Most news feeds are broken because they treat everyone the same.
body:
It is frustrating to open a news app and see headlines that have nothing to do with your life or your interests. The standard approach is to guess what you might like based on broad categories. But human curiosity is much more specific than a category like technology or politics.

We built Headlinne to change that. Instead of guessing, our recommendation engine works by paying attention to what you actually engage with over time. It learns from your habits. If you read a piece about urban planning in your city, it understands you are interested in local policy, not just generic city news. It is about nuance.

This is not about trapping you in a bubble. It is about cutting through the noise so you can find the stories that matter to you. When the technology works well, it feels less like an algorithm and more like a helpful editor who knows what you like to read.

We believe technology should save you time, not waste it.
cta: You can see how it works for yourself at HEADLINNE.com.
